frames: The agent is in a low, crouched posture with all four legs on the checkered ground, appearing stable.
It begins a subtle rocking motion, lifting its front-right leg slightly, then its front-left leg.
The rocking continues, with the front legs alternately lifting and touching down, but the agent remains largely stationary.
The agent shifts its weight, lifting its front-right leg again, then its front-left, maintaining a low, stable stance.
It lifts its front-right leg higher, then its front-left, and begins to lean forward, showing signs of instability.
The agent's body tilts sharply forward and to the left, losing balance as its front-left leg is raised.
It falls completely onto its back, with all four legs flailing upwards, now inverted and chaotic.
The agent remains on its back, legs splayed, unable to recover or perform any locomotion.
